name: Satet
description: Satet, also known as Satis or Satjet, was an ancient Egyptian goddess who personified the inundation of the Nile River and served as a guardian of the southern frontier of Egypt. She was particularly associated with the island of Elephantine near Aswan, where she formed part of a triad with Khnum, the potter god who created humans on his wheel, and their daughter Anuket, another goddess of the Nile. Satet's role was crucial to the prosperity of Egypt, as the annual Nile flood brought fertile silt that enriched the land for agriculture. She was depicted as a woman wearing the White Crown of Upper Egypt adorned with gazelle horns, symbolizing her southern origins and connection to the hunt. Her powers were connected to the life-giving and, at times, destructive force of the Nile, making her a deity to be both revered and respected. Satet was also seen as a skilled archer, protecting Egypt from enemies and ensuring the stability of the southern border. She was worshipped from the Predynastic Period through the Greco-Roman era, demonstrating her enduring importance in Egyptian religious beliefs. Offerings were made to her to ensure a bountiful flood and the continued fertility of the land. Temples dedicated to Satet have been found at Elephantine, highlighting the significance of her cult in that region. Her association with the Nile inundation connected her to themes of rebirth and renewal, further solidifying her place in the Egyptian pantheon as a vital force in the cycle of life.
subtitle: Goddess of the Nile inundation.

keyMyths.title: The Nile Inundation
keyMyths.description: Satet controls the annual flooding of the Nile, ensuring the fertility of the land. Her actions are vital for the prosperity of Egypt and its people.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of the South
keyMyths.description: Satet protects the southern border of Egypt, using her archery skills to ward off enemies. She maintains stability and order in the region.

keyMyths.title: Part of the Elephantine Triad
keyMyths.description: Satet, along with Khnum and Anuket, forms a powerful triad worshipped at Elephantine. They represent essential aspects of life and creation in Egypt.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
